display.purpose: The hero name only. Fraunces with optical sizing live.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The final CTA only. Solid orange with an ink mono label (AA-safe at 4.42:1).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--color-orange, #E9622E); color: var(--color-ink, #2A2722); font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 14px 28px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), filter 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); filter: brightness(0.96); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-orange, #E9622E); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Mosaic Tile
components.description: A composed puzzle piece. Hairline at rest; orange edge on hover when it links to a case study. No shadow.
components.html:
AI & Physics

How I think, in problems and proofs.
components.css: .ds-tile { background: var(--color-paper, #F4EFE6); color: var(--color-ink, #2A2722); border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-muted-light, #8A8377) 40%, transparent); border-radius: 12px; padding: 28px; transition: transform 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), border-color 300ms ease; } .ds-tile:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); border-color: var(--color-orange, #E9622E); } .ds-tile-title { font-family: 'Fraunces', ui-serif, Georgia, serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.5rem; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-tile-line { font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--color-muted-light, #8A8377); margin: 0; }

components.name: Ghost Link
components.description: The standard link. An orange underline draws in from the left on hover.
components.css: .ds-ghost { position: relative; color: inherit; text-decoration: none; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-ghost::after { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; bottom: -2px; height: 1px; width: 100%; background: var(--color-orange, #E9622E); transform: scaleX(0); transform-origin: left; transition: transform 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-ghost:hover::after { transform: scaleX(1); } .ds-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-orange, #E9622E); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Eyebrow
components.description: Mono, uppercase, tracked. Carries real data (coordinates, dates, altitude), never a decorative kicker.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--color-muted-light, #8A8377); }

narrative.overview: A cockpit at altitude is the whole system in one image: matte, disciplined surfaces; precise mono-spaced instruments reading real data; one warm signal light; and, beyond the glass, a sky that changes from day to night as you climb. The interface is calm and exact almost everywhere, so the one scenographic thing -- the tonal flight the background performs as you scroll -- lands like a view, not a gimmick. Depth comes from tone and atmosphere, not from drop shadows and glass.
keyCharacteristics: One accent (orange), one scenographic effect (the tonal flight), everything else quiet.
Mono type carries real data -- coordinates, dates, altitude, stack -- never decoration.
Editorial scale contrast: a large humanist serif against a precise grotesque body.
Atmosphere over elevation: depth from tonal layering and space, not shadows.
AA-or-better contrast is a hard floor; reduced motion is a first-class path.

rules.name: The Single Voice Rule
rules.body: Orange appears on a small fraction of any screen and is never diluted. Its rarity is the signal. If orange is doing decoration, remove it.

rules.name: The Blue-Black Rule
rules.body: The dark surface is #14161D (a cool blue-black sky), never a warm brown-black. The warm-brown night is the AI-generator tell this system exists to avoid.

rules.name: The Real-Data Rule
rules.body: The mono label carries true information -- coordinates, a date, an altitude, a stack -- never a decorative kicker. If the eyebrow doesn't say something true, it doesn't ship.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Tiles lift on hover with a small translateY and an orange hairline edge -- a response to state, not a resting elevation.

dos: Do keep orange to a small fraction of any view; let its rarity be the signal.
Do make every mono eyebrow carry true data (coordinates, dates, altitude, stack).
Do build depth from tone, space, and 1px hairlines -- atmosphere over elevation.
Do pair on the contrast axis: Fraunces serif against Geist grotesque.
Do give prefers-reduced-motion a real path: tonal shifts become instant, reveals off.
Do verify every text/background pair against AA; bump toward ink if it's even close.
donts: Don't ship the generic AI-generator look -- cream paper + humanist serif + terracotta accent.
Don't ship a stock portfolio template -- uniform identical-tile card grids with no hierarchy.
Don't build the template SaaS hero (big-number metric + gradient blob + generic CTA).
Don't add a dark/light toggle. Tone follows altitude; the journey is the theme.
Don't dilute orange into decorative fills, or use it as small body text.
Don't use box-shadow or glassmorphism to fake depth. Use tone and space.
Don't use cream text on the orange button (3.14:1, fails AA). Use ink.
